sentences.text: When you're born, they give you the name.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] When I was five years old, maybe.
And then she didn't like keeping-- keeping his name.
And everybody know in the city my name is [?
Three-- she was three years younger than I am.
He used to make fur coats.
Is not like here in United States.
They can't-- they don't give you any benefit.
I tell-- my mother's brother, he left-- he left-- he left to Johannesburg.
He was a good tailor in the city.
Then he was in Johannesburg, in Africa.
Then if you don't like there, you come back.
And then he said to my mother, I used to go to school before.
I used to go to Hebrew school.
Until later on, after I finish the school, Hebrew school, [? she went to ?] the public school.
And my father-- my-- my uncle comes back from Africa.
He said to my mother, why you have to learn-- [CRYING]
I'm going to teach him about tailor.
Then I started to go to for a tailor.
And all the time, they used to ask me, the boys, my friend, why you come back to school?
How old were you when you started to learn how to be a tailor?
Oh, I remember-- not good-- not very good.
I-- I never want to remember.
I don't like to remember any more.
[CRYING] See, that is all I think, [INAUDIBLE] before, cause I got nothing [INAUDIBLE] it.
And I get, you know, older, and started to work and learn a trade, and I started to make money.
Then my-- then I said to my mother and it broke her heart.
And my sister started to work.
She used to make bras-- how you call it-- for-- for a woman.
And she started to work, too.
[CRYING] And then, that is happen the trouble.
Oh, my mother used to-- religious-- we-- we are Jews and religious, very religious.
Yeah, she never said [INAUDIBLE] Saturdays, you cannot do it Fridays, Saturdays, not in Europe, difference.
She-- first of all, they never had electric over there.
Gas-- night-- different lamps, you know, you have to in, not gasoline.
That's-- that's always life in Poland.
Later on, when I started to work, it was different, all different.
--not for long, because after that, we were not far from the war.
You have to go some-- Yom Kippur.
Saturdays, my mother, even she left [? nothing ?] [INAUDIBLE].
Men-- used to go, in Poland, used to go [INAUDIBLE].
Poor men used to go for-- go around to every house, to give them a penny like this.
She go home, and go back, and take some of their own for eat.
She was-- she was very good.
[CRYING] That I can't ever forget it.
No, not Polish, most Jewish, all Jewish.
Even the public school, only Jews.
Polish were anti-Semite all the time.
We used to go [INAUDIBLE], all the group.
I used to a stick in my hand, put it here, because I'm afraid of the Polish.
It started before [? the war started, ?] the Nazis.
And the youth in Poland, we used to call them [NON-ENGLISH].
One time I used to go to work to my uncle, wasn't in-- in lunchtime, after we come home, going back, they run after me, with the Gestapo-- the Polish [NON-ENGLISH] with knives in hand.
I ran-- if I don't run, they will kill me.
I never want to go back after the war because of this.
I used to belong to a sport club.
Make sport, and then we make-- I don't know what they call it-- [INAUDIBLE] used to make-- not as a young man, not heavy, skinny.
I used to go on the top for somebody else in the-- in the-- in a pyramid.
Then I belonged-- here called soccer.
In Europe, they called it football, different.
I do a lot of things.
I used to make, like, make pictures, too, like I do now.
I used to make sometime, used to belong-- used to think, I can write good.
I used to print-- how you call  it, [NON-ENGLISH], how you call it in English-- on the streets, to make it, used to [INAUDIBLE].
I don't know how to call it.
Oh, I did with my uncle.
I cannot ?] explain it to you.
To start being a tailor is not so easy.
People think here, tailors are easy.
This not-- here, even Los Angeles, I'm not a tailor like this.
I show you my shop here.
I've got a little-- you see [? a ?] movies [INAUDIBLE].
These come here in the house.
I quit to come to do them.
You [INAUDIBLE] I'm talking about the war.
My uncle was the best tailor in the city.
And he said to me, he never-- [CRYING] Oh, I cannot talk [INAUDIBLE].
And goes back and back, then I see [INAUDIBLE].
You know the picture I show before, the people that are in Israel, my mother used to go work for them, too.
She used to come, said, your mother will leave now-- [CRYING] She was a [INAUDIBLE].
She said to me, if your mother would leave now, [NON-ENGLISH].
She was-- she was never-- [CRYING] oh, let me talk something else.
The said with 4,000 people there, more than just Jewish.
Was after the war, the other [INAUDIBLE]-- how you call, [NON-ENGLISH].
That's over 4,000 gone and dead in concentration camp.
Lot of them-- lot of them-- mostly Jewish.
I never think to pay attention.
In Europe, you don't care how many people.
You got your friend, you got a [NON-ENGLISH].
Mostly-- mostly Gentiles used to live out-- out of the city.
Oh, I don't care how many.
I never pay attention to this.
Oh, they used to-- used to send it out of [NON-ENGLISH], they call the camps the summertime, together.
Used to go all the time in meetings together, and talk about Israel, talk everythings.
Every-- every-- after work, right after work, we used to go to it.
I don't know what to call it in English.
I don't care because there's no organization [? here ?] too much.
If you want to see people, you have to go to the temple.
Or you got today, the [INAUDIBLE] club, the Survivor Club, that's it.
That's the only things you can go.
Otherwise, you have to sit in the house.
You see how many people show in the picture.
There was richer, and the poor men.
I used to go to work.
Used to work for other people, too, not just from my uncle.
Later on, used to go to other-- other tailors.
In the morning, I used to-- before I go to work, I used to read in paper, [? foreign ?] [? ones. ?]
Oh, all kind thing-- other things in and other wars that started before.
And then I-- one time I left the [INAUDIBLE], because I cannot work no more in the-- in home.
I was in [INAUDIBLE] about few months.
My mother had a brother in [INAUDIBLE], too.
Then they started the war Poland starting the war-- Germany against-- I was over there.
I forgot the name of it.
I was in [INAUDIBLE], working over there.
Then my uncle, other uncle, said to me, go home.
My mother will-- then I left back home.
They started wars with Poland, others.
Oh, there-- I saw-- a lot of Polish used to stand by the Jewish stores and never intending to buy something against the Jews.
Oh, that's [? terrible. ?] [INAUDIBLE] don't go to the Jews, don't go to the Jews, don't go to the Jews.
Is how right away, they run to every house and look for boys, for men, for men, for men-- go to work.
You never can sleep any more.
After-- after-- a while, [INAUDIBLE], they started curfew time.
After 6:00, you have to stay in the house.
That was worst thing for them to catch us.
Beginning, yes, [? off that bat. ?] Oh, then they started.
The [INAUDIBLE] the Polish [INAUDIBLE] do the thing.
Then they started to put on a band with a-- with a Jewish star.
And I used to go-- I used to take the shirts and cover this.
Then the Polish said, he's a juden.
What they-- then the Germans try to make a-- call up all the Jews, the richer Jews, and they make them to be the leaders.
If they need something, they have to-- they have to get it from the others.
All those names-- I-- I remember the names.
Because mostly they take the richer people.
Oh, some people was good, and some was bad.
Even they was richer, some was good and some was bad.
They take advantage of the-- of the poor people.
Did they need some to work?
I remember, some time ago, the poor children [INAUDIBLE].
In Jewish, they call the Judenrat.
Here, they call it the-- I don't know how they call it.
They used to go over there and-- and cry, why you send us for work?
[INAUDIBLE] others, you think the others are gonna stay alive?
Yeah, experience, because they want to take me to work all the time.
And I used to work with my uncle.
My mother said, no, you not going to work.
[CRYING] They sent my-- my sister-- my sister to work.
She said-- because if she goes, [INAUDIBLE].
My sister always used to call for me.
If I ever go, sometimes take away, you never come back.
And even though I said, no, I want for him to go, she said no, he's not going.
[PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] See, that's why I [? never ?] liked to act, said come like this still.
Then another thing with the Judenrat-- how call-- how you call the unit-- the [? committee, ?] [? committee ?] [INAUDIBLE].
They do something else, or they started to [INAUDIBLE] trouble.
Then I work later on with my uncle.
[INAUDIBLE] never [? can sometimes ?] [? get on ?] not good.
Then I went to other place work.
[INAUDIBLE] what used to belong to the community-- leaders from the-- from the Jews.
And I asked him, why you take [INAUDIBLE]?
[INAUDIBLE] the paper from the German Gestapo, and then after take me to work.
He did it for his friends and family.
Then I said, what to do?
Because I don't like, like this.
I went back to my uncle.
My uncle is the best tailor.
He used to work for the Gestapo.
And they said-- used to send it back to Germany.
I work for him long time.
[INAUDIBLE] all the time to make a transport out.
[NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] ?] Over there was the main-- main-- the main stop to send [INAUDIBLE] Auschwitz, Treblinka, other-- other places.
And then I said, long time I [INAUDIBLE] on the list.
It used to catch [INAUDIBLE] to work.
I know they knocking the door, in the night, [INAUDIBLE].
Because I told you, lived in our city-- in our place, [INAUDIBLE].
In the morning, on the hill-- on the [INAUDIBLE] cover.
And I said, you sit up.
That's how I hide myself like this.
That's not-- not-- was not close.
They don't go out because [INAUDIBLE] close.
They took away all the big-- big houses, the newer houses, for the army.
Just there were side streets just for the Jews.
And I see after there, they-- there are other times, they do-- they used to get people to work.
A long time after, I see the [INAUDIBLE].
I said to Mother, what can I do now?
Then I said to Mother, [INAUDIBLE] the floor or [INAUDIBLE] on the floor.
She covered it all-- took off in the bed, [INAUDIBLE] the floor, to show the people, the Germans, there was other Germans already messed us up.
And I was underneath, [? in the barn. ?]
Then when they went out, I said to Mother, come on.
I dress in for a woman.
My mother's-- and I walk out.
Another place, I work the assembly line.
I work-- I work the assembly place.
I learned to use-- on-- on-- on a roof.
A roof attic like this, is in over there two days, two days.
In a heat like this, it's summertime.
After a while, they said, they already-- they already left.
Course, I ask him in the Russian-- the Russian was not far from our city.
Zelig, ?] maybe you go with me to Russian?
Course he used to work for the Germans, too, [? and so on. ?] [INAUDIBLE] nothing, [INAUDIBLE] nothing.
He worked if they needed him [INAUDIBLE].
Later on, long time, I got [INAUDIBLE] on the girls.
Sitting-- that was a Saturday-- Saturday or Sunday, Sitting in our home, and talking, talking.
Then I see, come Sunday, The [INAUDIBLE], the Gestapo, they get on the-- there [INAUDIBLE].
Then I run off my attic, where I used to-- run, you know, outside was a ladder, and up and down.
And he saw me going down.
Then he hold a rifle, like this, run, go.
They were coming on our street.
There my-- my friend from [INAUDIBLE], they started talking about the girls.
They cannot catch me, and I run out. you were running and this-- Yes.
Zelig. ?] Then I hide, because I know the city.
And my mother ran after me.
And she-- she was so close to him.
And I hide in a-- in a shack.
Then after a while, my mother said to me, she won't-- she [? won't ?] talk to me.
And I know what she said.
Then I run out, go over-- over the roof to another place.
And go to my girlfriend, I used to go with her.
And then her brother-in-law's in our basement around him.
And after all, they run out in our city, this-- [? they said, ?] oh, he's still alive.
My mother doesn't believe it yet, but there's not [INAUDIBLE].
Then one night, everything else happened in not in same time.
Then that little door it open, open, never opens.
She was laying in bed with my mother, taking off the shirts.
And I was in other room.
I run out, and they run after me.
And I run of-- of the fields.
Across a gardens there, and run over there, to my-- to my cousin, different city, used to live on a gentile street.
And I said, [INAUDIBLE]-- this is a cousin, [INAUDIBLE].
She-- I said, my mother been dead.
My mother comes in the morning, and looks, and see they are still alive.
And then start over [? close, ?] and [? close. ?] And we come-- and I see my mother.
In the night, they knocked in the door to get everybody out.
[DRINKS] I'm all get dry when I'm excited too much what's happened.
Long time happen was less than [INAUDIBLE].
Then I make-- I make in a house another [INAUDIBLE], poor house.
Was a suitcases, everything's to [INAUDIBLE] floor.
I, with my cousin, my other cousin, dig a hole every night.
We make this-- here-- here was a table.
And you opened all under-- under the table.
They ran around to the barn.
And cover this, nobody will notice.
After all this, come to learn, too, they said they have to make a [INAUDIBLE].
Long time, and they knock the door, and took us all away.
Oh, yeah, there's not the girls.
They did-- the Judenrat, [? you see, ?] the-- the-- the community.
Because they have to get the Germans, how many people for a transport.
[INAUDIBLE] Gestapo, to Treblinka and other places.
They pick people that they like.
You know, they cannot afford to pay me.
They figured they got more money, maybe they can buy you off, not to go.
They put my mother on the list.
I look my mother on the list.
I said to my partner, to my uncle, I cannot see my [INAUDIBLE] sister.
He said, then he went to-- to leader for the community.
He said, why you took my sister?
Then he said to him, you kick him.
He said, you can kick him out.
Even though he works for the Gestapo, [INAUDIBLE].
He said, I'm going to Gestapo.
He said, why you send-- that is, he works for me.
Because-- he kicked him because he wanted to do it.
